item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: A Short Total Synthesis of the Daucane Sesquiterpenoid Ferutinin
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, EarlyView.
item.dc:description:
Only nine steps were required to access the bioactive daucane sesquiterpene ester ferutinin (1) from 3‐methyl‐2‐cyclopentenone (3) and allyl p‐tolyl sulfoxide. A one‐pot 1,4‐addition/aldol sequence was used to rapidly provide diene 2 (TBS = tert‐butyldimethylsilyl), ring‐closing metathesis of which followed by alkylation and acylation gave rise to the natural product 1 in high overall yield.
A straightforward total synthesis of the bioactive daucane sesquiterpene ester ferutinin is reported. Starting from 3‐methyl‐2‐cyclopentenone and allyl p‐tolyl sulfoxide, only nine steps were required to access the target sesquiterpenoid in 22% overall yield. A one‐pot sequential conjugate addition/aldol reaction to set up a trisubstituted cyclopentanone moiety and a ring‐closing metathesis to generate the hydroazulene core were used as the key transformations. Addition of isopropyllithium completed the daucane skeleton and eventually led to the sesquiterpene jaeschkeanadiol. Finally, esterification of this diol gave rise to ferutinin.
item.content:encoded:
Only nine steps were required to access the bioactive daucane sesquiterpene ester ferutinin (1) from 3-methyl-2-cyclopentenone (3) and allyl p-tolyl sulfoxide. A one-pot 1,4-addition/aldol sequence was used to rapidly provide diene 2 (TBS = tert-butyldimethylsilyl), ring-closing metathesis of which followed by alkylation and acylation gave rise to the natural product 1 in high overall yield.


A straightforward total synthesis of the bioactive daucane sesquiterpene ester ferutinin is reported. Starting from 3-methyl-2-cyclopentenone and allyl p-tolyl sulfoxide, only nine steps were required to access the target sesquiterpenoid in 22% overall yield. A one-pot sequential conjugate addition/aldol reaction to set up a trisubstituted cyclopentanone moiety and a ring-closing metathesis to generate the hydroazulene core were used as the key transformations. Addition of isopropyllithium completed the daucane skeleton and eventually led to the sesquiterpene jaeschkeanadiol. Finally, esterification of this diol gave rise to ferutinin.
item.dc:creator: Christiane Osthaar, Yuzhou Wang, Anne Jäger, Peter Metz
item.dc:title: A Short Total Synthesis of the Daucane Sesquiterpenoid Ferutinin

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Facile Synthesis of Spiro Trifluoromethyl Pyrazolone–Pyrazole Derivatives Through a Base‐Mediated (3+2) Cycloaddition
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, EarlyView.
item.dc:description:
CF3 ‐Pyrazolone. 1,3‐dipolar cycloaddition. Metal free conditions. Good yields. Broad substrate scope.
Herein, we describe a 1,3‐dipolar (3 + 2) cycloaddition between hydrazonoyl chlorides and substituted trifluoromethyl pyrazolones for the synthesis of spirotrifluoromethyl pyrazolone–pyrazole derivatives under transition‐metal‐free conditions. This protocol delivers CF3‐containing pyrazolone frameworks of notable synthetic and biological relevance. The cycloaddition enables the simultaneous formation of new CN and CC bonds, providing the desired spiro products in high yields. Moreover, the reaction demonstrates broad substrate compatibility, underscoring its practicality and synthetic utility.
item.content:encoded:
CF3 -Pyrazolone. 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition. Metal free conditions. Good yields. Broad substrate scope.


Herein, we describe a 1,3-dipolar (3 + 2) cycloaddition between hydrazonoyl chlorides and substituted trifluoromethyl pyrazolones for the synthesis of spirotrifluoromethyl pyrazolone–pyrazole derivatives under transition-metal-free conditions. This protocol delivers CF3-containing pyrazolone frameworks of notable synthetic and biological relevance. The cycloaddition enables the simultaneous formation of new CN and CC bonds, providing the desired spiro products in high yields. Moreover, the reaction demonstrates broad substrate compatibility, underscoring its practicality and synthetic utility.
item.dc:creator: Lingareddy Eeda, Susmitha Jadav, Sai Lokavarapu, Ravindra M. Kumbhare
item.dc:title: Facile Synthesis of Spiro Trifluoromethyl Pyrazolone–Pyrazole Derivatives Through a Base‐Mediated (3+2) Cycloaddition

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Activation of Aryl Halides by Triplet–Triplet Annihilation Upconversion Enabling Coupling Processes Under an Open‐to‐Air Environment
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, EarlyView.
item.dc:description:
Triplet–triplet annihilation upconversion (TTA‐UC) performed inside supramolecular viscoelastic gels enables efficient CC and C–heteroatom coupling reactions under green LED irradiation in open‐air conditions. The gel network restricts oxygen diffusion, preserves triplet states, and allows aryl halide activation through upconverted excited states, providing a robust and recyclable confined photoredox platform.
Herein, several CC and C–heteroatom coupling reactions were successfully achieved by combining triplet–triplet annihilation upconversion (TTA‐UC) photochemistry with supramolecular viscoelastic gels as confined media under green‐light LED irradiation. Unlike conventional TTA‐UC, which typically requires strict anaerobic conditions due to efficient oxygen quenching of triplet states, the present methodology operates under open‐to‐air conditions. The gel matrix, made of low‐molecular‐weight gelators, provides a nanostructured microenvironment that restricts oxygen diffusion and minimizes triplet‐oxygen collisional quenching, thereby preserving the efficiency of the upconversion process without additional additives. Using the well‐established PtOEP/DPA sensitizer–annihilator pair embedded within the gel network, aryl halides were activated through a photoinduced single‐electron transfer pathway enabled by the upconverted excited state. The method proved effective for a range of substrates affording the corresponding coupling products in moderate to good yields under mild conditions (ambient temperature, atmospheric pressure, visible light). Rheological and microscopic studies confirmed that the fibrillar gel architecture remains largely intact after irradiation, highlighting the mechanical robustness and recyclability of the confined medium for photoredox transformations.
item.content:encoded:
Triplet–triplet annihilation upconversion (TTA-UC) performed inside supramolecular viscoelastic gels enables efficient CC and C–heteroatom coupling reactions under green LED irradiation in open-air conditions. The gel network restricts oxygen diffusion, preserves triplet states, and allows aryl halide activation through upconverted excited states, providing a robust and recyclable confined photoredox platform.


Herein, several CC and C–heteroatom coupling reactions were successfully achieved by combining triplet–triplet annihilation upconversion (TTA-UC) photochemistry with supramolecular viscoelastic gels as confined media under green-light LED irradiation. Unlike conventional TTA-UC, which typically requires strict anaerobic conditions due to efficient oxygen quenching of triplet states, the present methodology operates under open-to-air conditions. The gel matrix, made of low-molecular-weight gelators, provides a nanostructured microenvironment that restricts oxygen diffusion and minimizes triplet-oxygen collisional quenching, thereby preserving the efficiency of the upconversion process without additional additives. Using the well-established PtOEP/DPA sensitizer–annihilator pair embedded within the gel network, aryl halides were activated through a photoinduced single-electron transfer pathway enabled by the upconverted excited state. The method proved effective for a range of substrates affording the corresponding coupling products in moderate to good yields under mild conditions (ambient temperature, atmospheric pressure, visible light). Rheological and microscopic studies confirmed that the fibrillar gel architecture remains largely intact after irradiation, highlighting the mechanical robustness and recyclability of the confined medium for photoredox transformations.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Álvarez‐Gutiérrez, Paola Domínguez Domínguez, M. Consuelo Jiménez, David Díaz Díaz, Raúl Pérez‐Ruiz
item.dc:title: Activation of Aryl Halides by Triplet–Triplet Annihilation Upconversion Enabling Coupling Processes Under an Open‐to‐Air Environment

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Copper/Photoredox‐Catalyzed Asymmetric 1,4‐Arylcyanation of 1,3‐Enynes to Access Chiral Tetrasubstituted Allenes
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, EarlyView.
item.dc:description:
A method for the photoredox/copper dual‐catalyzed asymmetric 1,4‐arylcyanation of 1,3‐enynes via a radical pathway has been established, in which Ar‐DBT+ salts serve as aryl radical precursors, affording diverse chiral tetra‐substituted allenes with moderate enantioselectivity and high chemo‐ and regioselectivity. This strategy operates under mild conditions, exhibits broad substrate scope with excellent functional group tolerance, and delivers chiral tetrasubstituted allenes in one step using both electron‐rich and electron‐deficient aryldibenzothiophenium (Ar‐DBT+) salts.
A method for the photoredox/copper dual‐catalyzed asymmetric 1,4‐arylcyanation of 1,3‐enynes via a radical pathway has been established, in which Ar‐DBT+ salts serve as aryl radical precursors, affording diverse chiral tetrasubstituted allenes with moderate enantioselectivity and high chemo‐ and regioselectivity. This strategy operates under mild conditions, exhibits broad substrate scope with excellent functional group tolerance, and delivers chiral tetra‐substituted allenes in one step using both electron‐rich and electron‐deficient aryldibenzothiophenium (Ar‐DBT+) salts.
item.content:encoded:
A method for the photoredox/copper dual-catalyzed asymmetric 1,4-arylcyanation of 1,3-enynes via a radical pathway has been established, in which Ar-DBT+ salts serve as aryl radical precursors, affording diverse chiral tetra-substituted allenes with moderate enantioselectivity and high chemo- and regioselectivity. This strategy operates under mild conditions, exhibits broad substrate scope with excellent functional group tolerance, and delivers chiral tetrasubstituted allenes in one step using both electron-rich and electron-deficient aryldibenzothiophenium (Ar-DBT+) salts.


A method for the photoredox/copper dual-catalyzed asymmetric 1,4-arylcyanation of 1,3-enynes via a radical pathway has been established, in which Ar-DBT+ salts serve as aryl radical precursors, affording diverse chiral tetrasubstituted allenes with moderate enantioselectivity and high chemo- and regioselectivity. This strategy operates under mild conditions, exhibits broad substrate scope with excellent functional group tolerance, and delivers chiral tetra-substituted allenes in one step using both electron-rich and electron-deficient aryldibenzothiophenium (Ar-DBT+) salts.
item.dc:creator: Xin‐Yu Peng, Wangqin Ji, Tao‐Yan Lin
item.dc:title: Copper/Photoredox‐Catalyzed Asymmetric 1,4‐Arylcyanation of 1,3‐Enynes to Access Chiral Tetrasubstituted Allenes

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Metal‐Free Synthesis of Allylic Phosphine Oxides via [2,3]‐Rearrangement/Michael Addition Cascade
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, EarlyView.
item.dc:description:
A novel metal‐free cascade synthesis of (E)‐2‐chloro‐allyl‐diarylphosphine oxides directly from propargylic alcohols and chlorodiarylphosphines was developed. This method efficiently combines phosphorylation, [2,3]‐sigmatropic rearrangement, and Michael addition in a single operation, achieving high stereocontrol and efficiency for the synthesis of allylic phosphine oxides.
Allylic phosphorous compounds are a class of important skeletons found in bioactive molecules and ligands. Here, we describe a synthesis of allylic phosphorous compounds from propargylic alcohols and chlorodiarylphosphines via a cascade reaction of OP bond formation/[2,3]‐rearrangement/Michael addition under metal‐free conditions. The reaction conditions are operationally simple and relatively mild, giving 2‐chloro‐allyl‐diarylphosphine oxides in (E)‐configuration selectively.
item.content:encoded:
A novel metal-free cascade synthesis of (E)-2-chloro-allyl-diarylphosphine oxides directly from propargylic alcohols and chlorodiarylphosphines was developed. This method efficiently combines phosphorylation, [2,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement, and Michael addition in a single operation, achieving high stereocontrol and efficiency for the synthesis of allylic phosphine oxides.


Allylic phosphorous compounds are a class of important skeletons found in bioactive molecules and ligands. Here, we describe a synthesis of allylic phosphorous compounds from propargylic alcohols and chlorodiarylphosphines via a cascade reaction of OP bond formation/[2,3]-rearrangement/Michael addition under metal-free conditions. The reaction conditions are operationally simple and relatively mild, giving 2-chloro-allyl-diarylphosphine oxides in (E)-configuration selectively.
item.dc:creator: Fangbing Chen, Junqing Liang, Atteeque Ahmed, Jiefeng Shen, Guoqiang Yang
item.dc:title: Metal‐Free Synthesis of Allylic Phosphine Oxides via [2,3]‐Rearrangement/Michael Addition Cascade

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Highly Stereoselective Synthesis of (E)‐(2‐Bromovinyl)selenoxides Using Solid Calcium Carbide as a Substitute for Gaseous Acetylene
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, EarlyView.
item.dc:description:
(E)‐(2‐Bromovinyl)selenoxides were concisely synthesized by a one‐pot procedure with high stereoselectivity using solid calcium carbide as an alkyne source instead of traditional gaseous acetylene, and solid tetrabutylammonium tribromide as a bromide source instead of traditional liquid bromine.
A concise method for the synthesis of (E)‐(2‐bromovinyl)arylselenoxides using calcium carbide as a solid alkyne source, tetrabutylammonium tribromide as a solid bromine source, benzoyl peroxide as an oxidant and an initiator, and 1,2‐diaryldiselenides as starting materials through a one‐pot procedure is described. The target products were obtained via cascade reactions involving the simultaneous formation of SeC, SeO, and CBr bonds. The salient features for this protocol are the use of inexpensive and easy‐to‐handle solid alkyne source instead of flammable and explosive gaseous acetylene, the use of safe and convenient solid bromine source instead of toxic and corrosive liquid bromine, high stereoselectivity, transition metal‐free conditions, wide functional tolerance, satisfactory yield, and simple workup procedures. The reactions can also be carried out on a gram scale.
item.content:encoded:
(E)-(2-Bromovinyl)selenoxides were concisely synthesized by a one-pot procedure with high stereoselectivity using solid calcium carbide as an alkyne source instead of traditional gaseous acetylene, and solid tetrabutylammonium tribromide as a bromide source instead of traditional liquid bromine.


A concise method for the synthesis of (E)-(2-bromovinyl)arylselenoxides using calcium carbide as a solid alkyne source, tetrabutylammonium tribromide as a solid bromine source, benzoyl peroxide as an oxidant and an initiator, and 1,2-diaryldiselenides as starting materials through a one-pot procedure is described. The target products were obtained via cascade reactions involving the simultaneous formation of SeC, SeO, and CBr bonds. The salient features for this protocol are the use of inexpensive and easy-to-handle solid alkyne source instead of flammable and explosive gaseous acetylene, the use of safe and convenient solid bromine source instead of toxic and corrosive liquid bromine, high stereoselectivity, transition metal-free conditions, wide functional tolerance, satisfactory yield, and simple workup procedures. The reactions can also be carried out on a gram scale.
item.dc:creator: Ting Shao, Botao Wang, Jinhui Yang, Zheng Li
item.dc:title: Highly Stereoselective Synthesis of (E)‐(2‐Bromovinyl)selenoxides Using Solid Calcium Carbide as a Substitute for Gaseous Acetylene

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Alkyl Arylacetates in Cascade Reactions with 2‐Cyanobenzaldehydes for the Synthesis of 3‐Substituted Isoindolinones
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, EarlyView.
item.dc:description:
Effective synthesis of 3‐substituted isoindolinones is possible through novel cascade reactions of alkyl arylacetates with 2‐cyanobenzaldehydes under mild, basic conditions.
Herein we report the use of alkyl arylacetates as pro‐nucleophiles in cascade reactions with 2‐cyanobenzaldehydes leading to the formation of novel 3‐substituted isoindolinones, a heterocyclic scaffold found in many natural products and bioactive synthetic compounds. The reaction proceeds under very mild conditions, generally affording good to excellent yields. We also demonstrated that diastereoselectivity is dependent on the presence of strong electron‐withdrawing substituents at the aromatic ring of the nucleophile. Kinetic investigations were performed by UV–vis stopped‐flow methods, indicating a clear dependence of the reaction rate on the nucleophilic reactivity of the carbanionic intermediates.
item.content:encoded:
Effective synthesis of 3-substituted isoindolinones is possible through novel cascade reactions of alkyl arylacetates with 2-cyanobenzaldehydes under mild, basic conditions.


Herein we report the use of alkyl arylacetates as pro-nucleophiles in cascade reactions with 2-cyanobenzaldehydes leading to the formation of novel 3-substituted isoindolinones, a heterocyclic scaffold found in many natural products and bioactive synthetic compounds. The reaction proceeds under very mild conditions, generally affording good to excellent yields. We also demonstrated that diastereoselectivity is dependent on the presence of strong electron-withdrawing substituents at the aromatic ring of the nucleophile. Kinetic investigations were performed by UV–vis stopped-flow methods, indicating a clear dependence of the reaction rate on the nucleophilic reactivity of the carbanionic intermediates.
item.dc:creator: Antonia Di Mola, Prabaharan Thiruvengetam, Alessandra Forlenza, Armin R. Ofial, Antonio Massa
item.dc:title: Alkyl Arylacetates in Cascade Reactions with 2‐Cyanobenzaldehydes for the Synthesis of 3‐Substituted Isoindolinones

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Construction of Cyclobutane‐Fused Quinolizine Frameworks Through Intramolecular [2 + 2] Cycloaddition of Reissert Compounds from Isoquinolines Catalyzed by an Iridium Visible‐Light Photocatalyst
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, EarlyView.
item.dc:description:
An unprecedented [2 + 2] photochemical cycloaddition of Reissert compounds catalyzed by visible‐light photocatalysts under blue light‐emitting diode irradiation (455 nm) is described. The reaction efficiently affords cyclobutane‐fused benzo[b]quinolizinone derivatives in high yields and with moderate to high stereoselectivity. A catalyst loading as low as 0.5 mol% is sufficient to drive the reaction to completion within 1 h in many cases.
Irradiation of 2‐(ω‐alkenoyl)‐1,2‐dihydroisoquinoline‐1‐carbonitriles (Reissert compounds) with blue light‐emitting diodes (λ = 455 nm) in the presence of an iridium‐based photocatalyst efficiently afforded cyclobutane‐fused benzo[b]quinolizinone derivatives in high yields and with moderate to high stereoselectivity. A catalyst loading as low as 0.5 mol% was sufficient to drive the reaction to completion within 1 h in many cases. Mechanistic studies suggest that the transformation proceeds via a stepwise [2 + 2] cycloaddition pathway involving a triplet biradical intermediate.
item.content:encoded:
An unprecedented [2 + 2] photochemical cycloaddition of Reissert compounds catalyzed by visible-light photocatalysts under blue light-emitting diode irradiation (455 nm) is described. The reaction efficiently affords cyclobutane-fused benzo[b]quinolizinone derivatives in high yields and with moderate to high stereoselectivity. A catalyst loading as low as 0.5 mol% is sufficient to drive the reaction to completion within 1 h in many cases.


Irradiation of 2-(ω-alkenoyl)-1,2-dihydroisoquinoline-1-carbonitriles (Reissert compounds) with blue light-emitting diodes (λ = 455 nm) in the presence of an iridium-based photocatalyst efficiently afforded cyclobutane-fused benzo[b]quinolizinone derivatives in high yields and with moderate to high stereoselectivity. A catalyst loading as low as 0.5 mol% was sufficient to drive the reaction to completion within 1 h in many cases. Mechanistic studies suggest that the transformation proceeds via a stepwise [2 + 2] cycloaddition pathway involving a triplet biradical intermediate.
item.dc:creator: Noriyoshi Arai, Tatsuro Imakubo, Nonoka Tajiri
item.dc:title: Construction of Cyclobutane‐Fused Quinolizine Frameworks Through Intramolecular [2 + 2] Cycloaddition of Reissert Compounds from Isoquinolines Catalyzed by an Iridium Visible‐Light Photocatalyst

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Pt/C‐Catalyzed Deuteration of Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons with Suppressed Arene Hydrogenation
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, EarlyView.
item.dc:description:
An additive‐enabled Pt/C protocol promotes hydrogen–deuterium exchange in fused polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) in D2O/2‐PrOH at 80 °C. Decalin suppresses undesired ring hydrogenation while accelerating on‐ring H–D exchange, furnishing highly deuterated PAHs (PAHs‐dn). This method provides practical access to deuterated π‐architectures and rigid scaffolds for mechanistic and photophysical studies.
Deuterated polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) are indispensable for investigating isotope effects in π‐conjugated materials; however, catalytic H–D exchange of readily hydrogenated PAHs is often compromised by competitive ring hydrogenation. This article reports a Pt/C‐catalyzed H–D exchange protocol employing inexpensive D2O as the deuterium source and 2‐propanol as the hydrogen donor under an Ar atmosphere. Additive screening identified decalin as a critical reaction modulator, enabling efficient deuterium incorporation while suppressing the formation of partially hydrogenated byproducts. Under the optimized conditions (80°C, 12 h), ≤97% deuterium incorporation was achieved for anthracene. Additionally, a range of fused PAHs, including 9‐aryl anthracenes, pyrene, triphenylene, and triptycene, were converted to their deuterated analogs in good‐to‐excellent isolated yields with negligible ring hydrogenation. This operationally simple method provides streamlined access to deuterated PAHs for materials‐oriented investigations and isotopic‐labeling applications.
item.content:encoded:
An additive-enabled Pt/C protocol promotes hydrogen–deuterium exchange in fused polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) in D2O/2-PrOH at 80 °C. Decalin suppresses undesired ring hydrogenation while accelerating on-ring H–D exchange, furnishing highly deuterated PAHs (PAHs-d n). This method provides practical access to deuterated π-architectures and rigid scaffolds for mechanistic and photophysical studies.


Deuterated polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) are indispensable for investigating isotope effects in π-conjugated materials; however, catalytic H–D exchange of readily hydrogenated PAHs is often compromised by competitive ring hydrogenation. This article reports a Pt/C-catalyzed H–D exchange protocol employing inexpensive D2O as the deuterium source and 2-propanol as the hydrogen donor under an Ar atmosphere. Additive screening identified decalin as a critical reaction modulator, enabling efficient deuterium incorporation while suppressing the formation of partially hydrogenated byproducts. Under the optimized conditions (80°C, 12 h), ≤97% deuterium incorporation was achieved for anthracene. Additionally, a range of fused PAHs, including 9-aryl anthracenes, pyrene, triphenylene, and triptycene, were converted to their deuterated analogs in good-to-excellent isolated yields with negligible ring hydrogenation. This operationally simple method provides streamlined access to deuterated PAHs for materials-oriented investigations and isotopic-labeling applications.
item.dc:creator: Naoya Sakurada, Sae Tomoto, Naoki Oka, Tomohiro Ichikawa, Takashi Shigeta, Hironao Sajiki, Takashi Ikawa
item.dc:title: Pt/C‐Catalyzed Deuteration of Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons with Suppressed Arene Hydrogenation

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Overcoming the Reactivity Challenge in Radical Fluoroalkylation of N‐Phenylhydrazones: A Divergent Approach for Substrates Derived from Aliphatic and Aromatic Aldehydes
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, EarlyView.
item.dc:description:
A photocatalytic method for the C–H perfluoroalkylation of N‐phenylhydrazones is described. The reaction works with perfluoroalkyl iodides and bromides as radical precursors, and the products can be transformed into various nitrogen heterocycles.
A photocatalytic method for the C–H perfluoroalkylation of N‐phenylhydrazones is described. The reaction effectively utilizes both fluoroalkyl iodides and bromides as radical precursors, which enables the introduction of a wide variety of fluoroalkyl fragments. Different reaction conditions were required for hydrazones derived from aliphatic and aromatic aldehydes. The obtained α‐perfluoroalkyl‐substituted hydrazones serve as precursors for the synthesis of diverse heterocycles bearing a fluoroalkyl group.
item.content:encoded:
A photocatalytic method for the C–H perfluoroalkylation of N-phenylhydrazones is described. The reaction works with perfluoroalkyl iodides and bromides as radical precursors, and the products can be transformed into various nitrogen heterocycles.


A photocatalytic method for the C–H perfluoroalkylation of N-phenylhydrazones is described. The reaction effectively utilizes both fluoroalkyl iodides and bromides as radical precursors, which enables the introduction of a wide variety of fluoroalkyl fragments. Different reaction conditions were required for hydrazones derived from aliphatic and aromatic aldehydes. The obtained α-perfluoroalkyl-substituted hydrazones serve as precursors for the synthesis of diverse heterocycles bearing a fluoroalkyl group.
item.dc:creator: German V. Svetlakov, Zakhar M. Rubanov, Vitalij V. Levin, Alexander D. Dilman
item.dc:title: Overcoming the Reactivity Challenge in Radical Fluoroalkylation of N‐Phenylhydrazones: A Divergent Approach for Substrates Derived from Aliphatic and Aromatic Aldehydes

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Synthesis of Oxazolyl‐Piperidines Bearing a Quaternary Stereocenter Through a Multicomponent Reaction Involving α‐Isocyanoacetates and Piperidones
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, EarlyView.
item.dc:description:
Straightforward access to oxazole‐containing piperidines through a multicomponent reaction from α‐isocyanoacetates is described. By expanding the reactivity of these a‐isocyanoacetates from aldehydes to ketones, this process efficiently delivers oxazolyl‐piperidines bearing a quaternary stereocenter as valuable scaffolds relevant to drug discovery.
The synthesis of 5‐alkyloxy‐oxazoles from α‐isocyanoacetates, aldehydes, and amines is well known, yet analogous reactions with ketones remain largely unexplored. Here, we investigate 3‐ and 4‐piperidones, selected for their biological relevance, in a multicomponent reaction with α‐(EWG‐phenyl)‐α‐isocyanoacetates and amines. This adapted multicomponent reaction (MCR) protocol provides an efficient, sustainable, and atom‐economic route to 4‐oxazolyl‐piperidines containing a quaternary stereocenter. The optimized conditions afford the desired heterocycles in good to excellent yields. Overall, this methodology expands the synthetic utility of isocyanoacetate‐based MCRs and delivers valuable scaffolds relevant to drug discovery.
item.content:encoded:
Straightforward access to oxazole-containing piperidines through a multicomponent reaction from α-isocyanoacetates is described. By expanding the reactivity of these a-isocyanoacetates from aldehydes to ketones, this process efficiently delivers oxazolyl-piperidines bearing a quaternary stereocenter as valuable scaffolds relevant to drug discovery.


The synthesis of 5-alkyloxy-oxazoles from α-isocyanoacetates, aldehydes, and amines is well known, yet analogous reactions with ketones remain largely unexplored. Here, we investigate 3- and 4-piperidones, selected for their biological relevance, in a multicomponent reaction with α-(EWG-phenyl)-α-isocyanoacetates and amines. This adapted multicomponent reaction (MCR) protocol provides an efficient, sustainable, and atom-economic route to 4-oxazolyl-piperidines containing a quaternary stereocenter. The optimized conditions afford the desired heterocycles in good to excellent yields. Overall, this methodology expands the synthetic utility of isocyanoacetate-based MCRs and delivers valuable scaffolds relevant to drug discovery.
item.dc:creator: Roshan Chandrakant Kajare, Gilles Argouarch, Nicolas Gouault, Claudia Lalli
item.dc:title: Synthesis of Oxazolyl‐Piperidines Bearing a Quaternary Stereocenter Through a Multicomponent Reaction Involving α‐Isocyanoacetates and Piperidones

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Visible Light or Brønsted Acid Promoted Oxygenation of a PdBn(NNP(O)) Complex
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, EarlyView.
item.dc:description:
A benzylpalladium complex with a tridentate NNP ligand containing 2,2′‐bipyridine and a phosphoryl moiety [PdBn(MeNNP(O))] reacted readily with O2 to afford an O2‐inserted complex. The insertion was accelerated using ambient light. By contrast, the reaction with O2 under blue LED irradiation afforded PhCHO as the major oxygenated product. The addition of acids accelerates oxygenation.
A benzylpalladium complex with a tridentate NNP ligand containing 2,2′‐bipyridine and a phosphoryl moiety, [PdBn(MeNNP(O))], was synthesized by reacting [{PdBn(MeNNP(O))}2H](OTf) with DBU. [PdBn(MeNNP(O))] readily reacted with O2 to afford [PdOOBn(MeNNP(O))], which was formed by O2 insertion into the Pd–C(sp3) bond. Although O2 insertion proceeded slowly in the dark, ambient light significantly accelerated insertion. Experimental results and density functional theory calculations showed that O2 insertion proceeded in the dark solely via a radical chain mechanism, whereas insertion under ambient light proceeded via both photoexcitation and radical chain mechanisms. By contrast, the reaction of [PdBn(MeNNP(O))] with O2 under blue LED irradiation afforded PhCHO as the major oxygenated product. Irradiation with blue LED light promoted the homolysis of the Pd–C(sp3) bond to afford a Pd(III) species and benzyl radical. The benzyl radical reacted with O2, and the subsequent hydrogen abstraction and dehydration afforded PhCHO. Moreover, the addition of acid accelerated the reaction of [PdBn(MeNNP(O))] with O2 in the dark, indicating that the phosphoryl moiety assisted the reaction with O2.
item.content:encoded:
A benzylpalladium complex with a tridentate NNP ligand containing 2,2′-bipyridine and a phosphoryl moiety [PdBn(MeNNP(O))] reacted readily with O2 to afford an O2-inserted complex. The insertion was accelerated using ambient light. By contrast, the reaction with O2 under blue LED irradiation afforded PhCHO as the major oxygenated product. The addition of acids accelerates oxygenation.


A benzylpalladium complex with a tridentate NNP ligand containing 2,2′-bipyridine and a phosphoryl moiety, [PdBn(MeNNP(O))], was synthesized by reacting [{PdBn(MeNNP(O))}2H](OTf) with DBU. [PdBn(MeNNP(O))] readily reacted with O2 to afford [PdOOBn(MeNNP(O))], which was formed by O2 insertion into the Pd–C(sp3) bond. Although O2 insertion proceeded slowly in the dark, ambient light significantly accelerated insertion. Experimental results and density functional theory calculations showed that O2 insertion proceeded in the dark solely via a radical chain mechanism, whereas insertion under ambient light proceeded via both photoexcitation and radical chain mechanisms. By contrast, the reaction of [PdBn(MeNNP(O))] with O2 under blue LED irradiation afforded PhCHO as the major oxygenated product. Irradiation with blue LED light promoted the homolysis of the Pd–C(sp3) bond to afford a Pd(III) species and benzyl radical. The benzyl radical reacted with O2, and the subsequent hydrogen abstraction and dehydration afforded PhCHO. Moreover, the addition of acid accelerated the reaction of [PdBn(MeNNP(O))] with O2 in the dark, indicating that the phosphoryl moiety assisted the reaction with O2.
item.dc:creator: Risa Shibai, Yuma Shigehiro, Shogo Sasaki, Yasuyuki Ura
item.dc:title: Visible Light or Brønsted Acid Promoted Oxygenation of a PdBn(NNP(O)) Complex

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Facile Access to Amides via Catalyst‐ and Additive‐Free Transamidation of N‐Acylpyrrole‐Type Amides
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, EarlyView.
item.dc:description:
A rapid synthesis of amides via transamidation of N‐acylpyrrole‐type amides is reported. This catalyst‐free protocol features mild reaction conditions, operational simplicity, and a broad substrate scope. Notably, hydroxylamines, which represent a class of nucleophiles rarely utilized in transamidation reactions, were successfully accommodated. The present strategy not only enriches the existing methodologies for amide synthesis but also expands the application scope of catalyst‐free transamidation reactions.
Amides are key structural units in organic synthesis, essential for the efficient production of many pharmaceuticals and industrial chemicals. Transamidation enables one‐pot conversion between different amides. However, conventional methods are often hampered by toxic/corrosive reagents, strong acids/bases, or metal‐mediated conditions, which can lead to undesired hydrolysis. Here, a catalyst‐ and additive‐free transamidation of N‐acylpyrrole‐type amides was developed. This method features mild conditions, operational simplicity, broad substrate scope, and excellent functional group compatibility.
item.content:encoded:
A rapid synthesis of amides via transamidation of N-acylpyrrole-type amides is reported. This catalyst-free protocol features mild reaction conditions, operational simplicity, and a broad substrate scope. Notably, hydroxylamines, which represent a class of nucleophiles rarely utilized in transamidation reactions, were successfully accommodated. The present strategy not only enriches the existing methodologies for amide synthesis but also expands the application scope of catalyst-free transamidation reactions.


Amides are key structural units in organic synthesis, essential for the efficient production of many pharmaceuticals and industrial chemicals. Transamidation enables one-pot conversion between different amides. However, conventional methods are often hampered by toxic/corrosive reagents, strong acids/bases, or metal-mediated conditions, which can lead to undesired hydrolysis. Here, a catalyst- and additive-free transamidation of N-acylpyrrole-type amides was developed. This method features mild conditions, operational simplicity, broad substrate scope, and excellent functional group compatibility.
item.dc:creator: Peng‐Fei Yang, Man‐Li Feng, Li Li, Xiu‐Li Zhang
item.dc:title: Facile Access to Amides via Catalyst‐ and Additive‐Free Transamidation of N‐Acylpyrrole‐Type Amides

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: HFIP‐Mediated Electrophilic Chemistry: Synthetic Utility and Mechanistic Insights
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, EarlyView.
item.dc:description:
HFIP: A remarkable multifunctional solvent for Electrophilic chemistry and for stabilization of cationic intermediates.
Hexafluoroisopropanol has emerged as a distinctive solvent, recognized for its ability to facilitate a variety of difficult chemical transformations. Its application has extended beyond conventional roles, serving effectively as a solvent, cosolvent, additive, and acidic promoter in organic synthesis. The compound's exceptional characteristics, particularly its high polarity and minimal nucleophilicity, enable the stabilization of cationic intermediates from a variety of sources, making it especially valuable in reactions involving electrophilic species.
item.content:encoded:
HFIP: A remarkable multifunctional solvent for Electrophilic chemistry and for stabilization of cationic intermediates.


Hexafluoroisopropanol has emerged as a distinctive solvent, recognized for its ability to facilitate a variety of difficult chemical transformations. The compound's exceptional characteristics, particularly its high polarity and minimal nucleophilicity, enable the stabilization of cationic intermediates from a variety of sources, making it especially valuable in reactions involving electrophilic species.

item.dc:title: HFIP‐Mediated Electrophilic Chemistry: Synthetic Utility and Mechanistic Insights

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Total Synthesis of Koshidacin A: Payne Rearrangement–Driven Enantioselective Construction of the Side Chain Triol Unit
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, Volume 29, Issue 19, 22 May 2026.
item.dc:description:
A stereoselective route for the synthesis of a fungal cyclic tetrapeptide, koshidacin A, is reported. The unusual hydrophilic amino acid residue present in koshidacin A was synthesized from (S)‐homo‐Garner aldehyde using Sharpless asymmetric epoxidation and Payne rearrangement as the key steps.
Koshidacin A is a cyclic tetrapeptide isolated from the Okinawan fungus, Pochonia boninensis FKR‐0564, along with its oxidized form, Koshidacin B. These peptides fall into a series of cyclic tetrapeptides made of three hydrophobic amino acids and a fourth amino acid with a hydrophilic side chain. We report a total synthesis of Koshidacin A, which relies on the asymmetric synthesis of the hydrophilic amino acid from (S)‐homo‐Garner aldehyde and using a combination of Sharpless asymmetric epoxidation and Payne rearrangement as the key steps.
item.content:encoded:
A stereoselective route for the synthesis of a fungal cyclic tetrapeptide, koshidacin A, is reported. The unusual hydrophilic amino acid residue present in koshidacin A was synthesized from (S)-homo-Garner aldehyde using Sharpless asymmetric epoxidation and Payne rearrangement as the key steps.


Koshidacin A is a cyclic tetrapeptide isolated from the Okinawan fungus, Pochonia boninensis FKR-0564, along with its oxidized form, Koshidacin B. These peptides fall into a series of cyclic tetrapeptides made of three hydrophobic amino acids and a fourth amino acid with a hydrophilic side chain. We report a total synthesis of Koshidacin A, which relies on the asymmetric synthesis of the hydrophilic amino acid from (S)-homo-Garner aldehyde and using a combination of Sharpless asymmetric epoxidation and Payne rearrangement as the key steps.
item.dc:creator: Preeti Kapri, Devki Nandan, Ramesh Ramapanicker
item.dc:title: Total Synthesis of Koshidacin A: Payne Rearrangement–Driven Enantioselective Construction of the Side Chain Triol Unit

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Metal‐Ligand Cooperative Cobalt (II) Catalysts for Synthesis of Triaryl Pyrazolines and Bis(indolyl)methanes via Alcohol Dehydrogenation
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, Volume 29, Issue 19, 22 May 2026.
item.dc:description:
In this study, the cobalt‐catalyzed synthesis of pyrazolines and bis(indolyl)methanes via dehydrogenation of primary alcohols was examined. The control experiments and detection of key intermediates via UV–vis, high resolution mass spectrometry, and IR spectroscopy indicated the involvement of the metal ligand cooperative approach.
In this study, the catalytic activity of cobalt complexes (Cat‐1 and Cat‐2) based on tetrazine ligand was examined for the synthesis of 1,3,5‐triaryl‐substituted 2‐pyrazolines via a multicomponent strategy and the coupling of alcohols with indoles to produce bis(indolyl)methanes (BIMs). The method utilizes alcohols as green alkylating agents under dehydrogenative conditions, offering a sustainable and atom‐economical approach. The methodology exhibits a wide substrate scope of 82 examples showing excellent functional group tolerance, including 13 bioactive compounds. Control experiments studies combined with the detection of key intermediates through UV–vis, fourier‐transform infrared spectroscopy, and high resolution mass spectrometry (HRMS) suggested the involvement of metal‐ligand cooperation in the plausible reaction pathway.
item.content:encoded:
In this study, the cobalt-catalyzed synthesis of pyrazolines and bis(indolyl)methanes via dehydrogenation of primary alcohols was examined. The control experiments and detection of key intermediates via UV–vis, high resolution mass spectrometry, and IR spectroscopy indicated the involvement of the metal ligand cooperative approach.


In this study, the catalytic activity of cobalt complexes (Cat-1 and Cat-2) based on tetrazine ligand was examined for the synthesis of 1,3,5-triaryl-substituted 2-pyrazolines via a multicomponent strategy and the coupling of alcohols with indoles to produce bis(indolyl)methanes (BIMs). The method utilizes alcohols as green alkylating agents under dehydrogenative conditions, offering a sustainable and atom-economical approach. The methodology exhibits a wide substrate scope of 82 examples showing excellent functional group tolerance, including 13 bioactive compounds. Control experiments studies combined with the detection of key intermediates through UV–vis, fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy, and high resolution mass spectrometry (HRMS) suggested the involvement of metal-ligand cooperation in the plausible reaction pathway.

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Copper‐Catalyzed Annulation Toward Fluoroalkylated Quinoxalines Using o‐Phenylenediamines and α‐Fluoroacetyl Diazoester
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, Volume 29, Issue 19, 22 May 2026.
item.dc:description:
A copper‐catalyzed cyclization provides efficient access to fluorinated quinoxalines from o‐phenylenediamines and diazodicarbonyl compounds. Proceeding under mild conditions with broad substrate scope, this method delivers diverse derivatives in moderate to good yields, offering a streamlined approach to scaffolds valuable for medicinal and materials science applications.

Proceeding under mild conditions with broad substrate scope, this protocol delivers diverse derivatives in moderate to good yields, offering a streamlined approach to scaffolds valuable for medicinal and materials science applications.

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Pd(II)/Ce(III)‐Heterobimetallic Complex‐Catalyzed Decarbonylative Alkyne Annulation Reaction: Synthesis of Spiro‐Benzofuranindenones
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, Volume 29, Issue 19, 22 May 2026.
item.dc:description:
A Pd(II) and Ce(III)‐catalyzed decarbonylative alkyne annulation reaction was developed for the efficient synthesis of spiro‐benzofuranindenones.
The decarbonylation of 3‐hydroxy‐2‐phenyl‐chromenones and subsequent annulation with disubstituted alkyne in the presence of Pd(OAc)2 (5.0 mol%) and Ce(OAc)3 (10.0 mol%) provided an efficient route for the synthesis of spiro‐benzofuranindenones. This reaction could be used for the synthesis of wide range of spiro‐benzofuranindenones with good yields.
item.content:encoded:
A Pd(II) and Ce(III)-catalyzed decarbonylative alkyne annulation reaction was developed for the efficient synthesis of spiro-benzofuranindenones.


The decarbonylation of 3-hydroxy-2-phenyl-chromenones and subsequent annulation with disubstituted alkyne in the presence of Pd(OAc)2 (5.0 mol%) and Ce(OAc)3 (10.0 mol%) provided an efficient route for the synthesis of spiro-benzofuranindenones. This reaction could be used for the synthesis of wide range of spiro-benzofuranindenones with good yields.
item.dc:creator: Bondana Bora, Kongkona Gogoi, Sanjib Gogoi
item.dc:title: Pd(II)/Ce(III)‐Heterobimetallic Complex‐Catalyzed Decarbonylative Alkyne Annulation Reaction: Synthesis of Spiro‐Benzofuranindenones

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Total Synthesis of Corynanthe Alkaloids: Corynantheol, Isocorynantheol, and Dihydrocorynantheol
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, Volume 29, Issue 19, 22 May 2026.
item.dc:description:
A synthetic method that is based on the Ireland‐Claisen rearrangement and the Pictet‐Spengler reaction as key steps to access an advanced intermediate of corynanthe alkaloids has been developed. The versatility of this approach is demonstrated through the racemic synthesis of several alkaloids: corynantheol, isocorynantheol, dihydrocorynantheol, 15‐epi‐corynantheol, and 3‐epi‐corynantheidol.
Here, we present a new synthetic approach to an advanced intermediate of the Corynanthe alkaloids, employing the Ireland‐Claisen rearrangement (ICR) and the Pictet‐Spengler reaction (PSR) as key transformations. Furthermore, as a representative application of this strategy, we accomplished the racemic total synthesis of corynanthe‐related alkaloids, including corynantheol, isocorynantheol, dihydrocorynantheol, 15‐epi‐corynantheol, and 3‐epi‐corynantheidol. This work underscores the versatility and synthetic utility of our approach within the broader context of the corynanthe alkaloid class.
item.content:encoded:
A synthetic method that is based on the Ireland-Claisen rearrangement and the Pictet-Spengler reaction as key steps to access an advanced intermediate of corynanthe alkaloids has been developed. The versatility of this approach is demonstrated through the racemic synthesis of several alkaloids: corynantheol, isocorynantheol, dihydrocorynantheol, 15-epi-corynantheol, and 3-epi-corynantheidol.


Here, we present a new synthetic approach to an advanced intermediate of the Corynanthe alkaloids, employing the Ireland-Claisen rearrangement (ICR) and the Pictet-Spengler reaction (PSR) as key transformations. Furthermore, as a representative application of this strategy, we accomplished the racemic total synthesis of corynanthe-related alkaloids, including corynantheol, isocorynantheol, dihydrocorynantheol, 15-epi-corynantheol, and 3-epi-corynantheidol. This work underscores the versatility and synthetic utility of our approach within the broader context of the corynanthe alkaloid class.

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Samarium(II) Ate Species Combined With Aminoalcohols as Reductants for Hydrogenation of Unsaturated Organic Compounds
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, Volume 29, Issue 19, 22 May 2026.
item.dc:description:
Samarium(II) ate species generated from SmI2, 3 equivalents of nBu4NCl, and a slight excess of L1H2 served as a superior reductant for reduction of 1‐substituted naphthalene derivatives, whose reactivity was higher than that with 2 equivalents of nBu4NCl or without nBu4NCl.
We explored the samarium(II)‐mediated reduction of unsaturated organic compounds using aminoalcohols as protic ligands. By screening various anionic (pseudo)halide ligands and their stoichiometry at the samarium(II) center, we found that samarium(II) ate species coordinated by chloride ligands exhibited superior reactivity. Furthermore, hydroxyethyl‐substituted ethylenediamine proved to be an effective supporting ligand as well as a proton source for the reduction of unsaturated organic compounds. UV–vis analyses of the anion exchange between SmI2 and halide sources (2 vs. 3 equiv.) revealed distinct absorption spectra depending on the amount of chloride, whereas bromide showed no significant spectral change.
item.content:encoded:
Samarium(II) ate species generated from SmI2, 3 equivalents of nBu4NCl, and a slight excess of L 1 H2 served as a superior reductant for reduction of 1-substituted naphthalene derivatives, whose reactivity was higher than that with 2 equivalents of nBu4NCl or without nBu4NCl.


We explored the samarium(II)-mediated reduction of unsaturated organic compounds using aminoalcohols as protic ligands. By screening various anionic (pseudo)halide ligands and their stoichiometry at the samarium(II) center, we found that samarium(II) ate species coordinated by chloride ligands exhibited superior reactivity. Furthermore, hydroxyethyl-substituted ethylenediamine proved to be an effective supporting ligand as well as a proton source for the reduction of unsaturated organic compounds. UV–vis analyses of the anion exchange between SmI2 and halide sources (2 vs. 3 equiv.) revealed distinct absorption spectra depending on the amount of chloride, whereas bromide showed no significant spectral change.
item.dc:creator: Yusuke Oda, Kulsum Bano, Hayato Tsurugi
item.dc:title: Samarium(II) Ate Species Combined With Aminoalcohols as Reductants for Hydrogenation of Unsaturated Organic Compounds

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Design and Synthesis of Lactose‐Derived Ligands and Their Application in Asymmetric Friedel–Crafts Alkylation
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, Volume 29, Issue 19, 22 May 2026.
item.dc:description:
This work describes the synthesis of lactose‐ and glucose‐derived chiral ligands and their application in the catalytic enantioselective Friedel–Crafts alkylation of indole with a series of trans‐β‐nitrostyrenes. The optimal ligand was an acetate protected, lactose‐derived pyridyl imine and its zinc complex afforded ees up to 93% and yields up to 93%. Additionally, a γ,β‐unsaturated‐α‐ketoester was employed in the Friedel–Crafts alkylation with indole, resulting in a 95% ee and 100% conversion.
D‐Lactose is a multi‐tonne waste product from the dairy industry, it is enantiopure and therefore of interest for potential use as a key component in chiral ligands for asymmetric catalysis. Owing to the development of successful carbohydrate‐containing ligands in the past, we now report the development of a series of bi‐ and tridentate lactose‐derived ligands with a variety of protecting groups, all of which are accessible in 3–5 synthetic steps. This is a novel application of lactose, as most carbohydrate ligands applied in metal‐catalysed asymmetric transformations are monosaccharides and none contain a lactose moiety. The lactose‐derived ligands prepared were applied in the asymmetric Friedel–Crafts alkylation of indole with various trans‐β‐nitrostyrenes affording ees up to 93% and yields up to 93%. Additionally, a γ,β‐unsaturated‐α‐ketoester was employed in the Friedel–Crafts alkylation with indole, resulting in a 95% ee and 100% conversion. To probe the functionality of the galactose unit of the lactose ligand, we applied an analogous glucose ligand in the same transformation furnishing inferior results of up to 82% ee and up to 66% yield. This demonstrates the advantage of using a disaccharide ligand.
item.content:encoded:
This work describes the synthesis of lactose- and glucose-derived chiral ligands and their application in the catalytic enantioselective Friedel–Crafts alkylation of indole with a series of trans-β-nitrostyrenes. The optimal ligand was an acetate protected, lactose-derived pyridyl imine and its zinc complex afforded ees up to 93% and yields up to 93%. Additionally, a γ,β-unsaturated-α-ketoester was employed in the Friedel–Crafts alkylation with indole, resulting in a 95% ee and 100% conversion.


D-Lactose is a multi-tonne waste product from the dairy industry, it is enantiopure and therefore of interest for potential use as a key component in chiral ligands for asymmetric catalysis. Owing to the development of successful carbohydrate-containing ligands in the past, we now report the development of a series of bi- and tridentate lactose-derived ligands with a variety of protecting groups, all of which are accessible in 3–5 synthetic steps. This is a novel application of lactose, as most carbohydrate ligands applied in metal-catalysed asymmetric transformations are monosaccharides and none contain a lactose moiety. The lactose-derived ligands prepared were applied in the asymmetric Friedel–Crafts alkylation of indole with various trans-β-nitrostyrenes affording ees up to 93% and yields up to 93%. Additionally, a γ,β-unsaturated-α-ketoester was employed in the Friedel–Crafts alkylation with indole, resulting in a 95% ee and 100% conversion. To probe the functionality of the galactose unit of the lactose ligand, we applied an analogous glucose ligand in the same transformation furnishing inferior results of up to 82% ee and up to 66% yield. This demonstrates the advantage of using a disaccharide ligand.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Bulawa, Julia Bruno‐Colmenarez, Patrick J. Guiry
item.dc:title: Design and Synthesis of Lactose‐Derived Ligands and Their Application in Asymmetric Friedel–Crafts Alkylation

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Synthesis of Eight‐Membered Carbocycle‐Fused 1,2,3‐Triazole Scaffolds via Palladium‐Catalyzed Tandem Reactions and Michael Addition
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, Volume 29, Issue 19, 22 May 2026.
item.dc:description:
A palladium‐catalyzed tandem reaction of 2‐iodophenyl‐enones and propargylic ethers is developed to provide the rapid construction of a fused eight‐membered carbocyclic ring‐1,2,3‐triazole scaffold. Furthermore, we demonstrate that subsequent base addition plays a crucial role in converting the Sonogashira cross‐coupling intermediate into the target product.
Both eight‐membered carbocyclic rings and 1,2,3‐triazole rings represent vital structural motifs. In this article, we present a Pd‐catalyzed tandem reaction that facilitates the synthesis of fused eight membered carbocyclic ring‐fused 1,2,3‐triazole derivatives from 2‐iodophenyl‐enones and propargylic ethers bearing azidophenyl group. Furthermore, we show that TBD plays a pivotal role as a mediator in converting the Sonogashira cross‐coupling intermediates into the desired products.
item.content:encoded:
A palladium-catalyzed tandem reaction of 2-iodophenyl-enones and propargylic ethers is developed to provide the rapid construction of a fused eight-membered carbocyclic ring-1,2,3-triazole scaffold. Furthermore, we demonstrate that subsequent base addition plays a crucial role in converting the Sonogashira cross-coupling intermediate into the target product.


Both eight-membered carbocyclic rings and 1,2,3-triazole rings represent vital structural motifs. In this article, we present a Pd-catalyzed tandem reaction that facilitates the synthesis of fused eight membered carbocyclic ring-fused 1,2,3-triazole derivatives from 2-iodophenyl-enones and propargylic ethers bearing azidophenyl group. Furthermore, we show that TBD plays a pivotal role as a mediator in converting the Sonogashira cross-coupling intermediates into the desired products.
item.dc:creator: Xinxin Chen, Yu Xin, Yuxuan Fang, Bin Liu, Ruwei Shen, Shugao Zhu
item.dc:title: Synthesis of Eight‐Membered Carbocycle‐Fused 1,2,3‐Triazole Scaffolds via Palladium‐Catalyzed Tandem Reactions and Michael Addition

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Visible‐Light‐Promoted, (Photo)catalyst‐Free Synthesis of 3‐Sulfonyl‐Azaspiro[4.5]trienones via Arylazo Sulfones
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, Volume 29, Issue 19, 22 May 2026.
item.dc:description:
A set of 3‐sulfonyl‐azaspiro[4.5]trienones have been obtained via a metal‐ and photocatalyst‐free visible‐light‐driven dearomative strategy. The protocol exploits arylazo sulfones, which act simultaneously as the light absorber and sulfonyl‐radical source, enabling ipso‐spirocyclization of differently substituted N‐aryl propiolamides.
Here, 3‐sulfonyl‐azaspiro[4.5]trienones are now accessible via a versatile visible‐light‐driven dearomative strategy under metal‐ and (photo)catalyst‐free conditions, starting from differently substituted N‐aryl propiolamides and arylazo sulfones. The protocol employs ethyl acetate as a benign solvent and tert‐butyl hydroperoxide as the oxidant. The reaction proceeds through direct photoexcitation of the arylazo sulfone, which acts simultaneously as a light absorber and a sulfonyl‐radical source, enabling ipso‐spirocyclization of aromatic amides. This transformation combines a broad substrate scope with mild conditions and operational simplicity, providing a valuable synthetic entry to sulfur‐containing spiroheterocycles of pharmacological interest.
item.content:encoded:
A set of 3-sulfonyl-azaspiro[4.5]trienones have been obtained via a metal- and photocatalyst-free visible-light-driven dearomative strategy. The protocol exploits arylazo sulfones, which act simultaneously as the light absorber and sulfonyl-radical source, enabling ipso-spirocyclization of differently substituted N-aryl propiolamides.


Here, 3-sulfonyl-azaspiro[4.5]trienones are now accessible via a versatile visible-light-driven dearomative strategy under metal- and (photo)catalyst-free conditions, starting from differently substituted N-aryl propiolamides and arylazo sulfones. The protocol employs ethyl acetate as a benign solvent and tert-butyl hydroperoxide as the oxidant. The reaction proceeds through direct photoexcitation of the arylazo sulfone, which acts simultaneously as a light absorber and a sulfonyl-radical source, enabling ipso-spirocyclization of aromatic amides. This transformation combines a broad substrate scope with mild conditions and operational simplicity, providing a valuable synthetic entry to sulfur-containing spiroheterocycles of pharmacological interest.

item.dc:title: Visible‐Light‐Promoted, (Photo)catalyst‐Free Synthesis of 3‐Sulfonyl‐Azaspiro[4.5]trienones via Arylazo Sulfones

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Base‐Catalyzed Sulfur‐Promoted Decarboxylative Insertion of Isothiocyanates into Isatin Framework: Multicomponent Access to Quinazoline‐2,4(1H,3H)‐Dithiones
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, Volume 29, Issue 19, 22 May 2026.
item.dc:description:
A base‐catalyzed and elemental sulfur‐promoted decarboxylative insertion of a CN motif of isothiocyanates into an isatin ring to construct quinazoline‐2,4(1H,3H)‐dithione has been described. This approach starts from easily available isatins, isothiocyanates, and sulfur, affording a series of structurally valuable heterocycles in satisfactory yields under mild conditions.
A base‐catalyzed and elemental sulfur‐promoted decarboxylative insertion of a CN motif of isothiocyanates into an isatin ring to construct quinazoline‐2,4(1H,3H)‐dithione has been described. This approach starts from easily available isatins, isothiocyanates, and sulfur, affording a series of structurally valuable heterocycles in satisfactory yields under mild conditions. Mechanism studies indicate that a base is essential to mediate the ring‐opening and thio‐Baeyer–Villiger rearrangement of isatin ring to pave the way for subsequent isothiocyanate insertion.
item.content:encoded:
A base-catalyzed and elemental sulfur-promoted decarboxylative insertion of a CN motif of isothiocyanates into an isatin ring to construct quinazoline-2,4(1H,3H)-dithione has been described. This approach starts from easily available isatins, isothiocyanates, and sulfur, affording a series of structurally valuable heterocycles in satisfactory yields under mild conditions.


A base-catalyzed and elemental sulfur-promoted decarboxylative insertion of a CN motif of isothiocyanates into an isatin ring to construct quinazoline-2,4(1H,3H)-dithione has been described. This approach starts from easily available isatins, isothiocyanates, and sulfur, affording a series of structurally valuable heterocycles in satisfactory yields under mild conditions. Mechanism studies indicate that a base is essential to mediate the ring-opening and thio-Baeyer–Villiger rearrangement of isatin ring to pave the way for subsequent isothiocyanate insertion.
item.dc:creator: Le Anh Nguyen, Quoc Trung Pham, Quoc Anh Ngo, Dinh Hung Mac, Thi Thu Tram Nguyen, Pascal Retailleau, Thanh Binh Nguyen
item.dc:title: Base‐Catalyzed Sulfur‐Promoted Decarboxylative Insertion of Isothiocyanates into Isatin Framework: Multicomponent Access to Quinazoline‐2,4(1H,3H)‐Dithiones

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Stereoselective Total Synthesis of All (8E)‐Stereoisomers of Panaxjapyne B
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, Volume 29, Issue 19, 22 May 2026.
item.dc:description:
Stereoselective total synthesis of all four possible (8E)‐stereoisomers of panaxjapyne B were accomplished and the comparative analysis revealed three of the synthesized products correspond to the natural products isolated from various sources. The synthesis involves copper(I) mediated regio‐selective ring opening of (R)‐ and (S)‐ TBS glycidyl ethers, base‐induced elimination of β‐alkoxy iodide and Cadiot‐Chodkiewicz cross‐coupling reaction as the key steps.
The first stereoselective total synthesis of all possible (8E)‐stereoisomers of panaxjapyne B was accomplished in a convergent fashion. The synthesis involves copper(I)‐mediated regioselective Grignard opening of (R)‐ and (S)‐OTBS glycidyl ethers, base‐induced elimination of β‐alkoxy iodides followed by Cadiot–Chodkiewicz cross‐coupling reaction as the key steps. D‐(−)‐Diethyl tartrate and both (R)‐ and (S)‐OTBS glycidyl ethers were used as chiral pool starting materials for the construction of the crucial chiral ene‐yne and chiral bromoalkyne fragments. Comparative analysis revealed that, out of all four possible (8E)‐stereoisomers of panaxjapyne B, three isomers correspond to natural products.
item.content:encoded:
Stereoselective total synthesis of all four possible (8E)-stereoisomers of panaxjapyne B were accomplished and the comparative analysis revealed three of the synthesized products correspond to the natural products isolated from various sources. The synthesis involves copper(I) mediated regio-selective ring opening of (R)- and (S)- TBS glycidyl ethers, base-induced elimination of β-alkoxy iodide and Cadiot-Chodkiewicz cross-coupling reaction as the key steps.


The first stereoselective total synthesis of all possible (8E)-stereoisomers of panaxjapyne B was accomplished in a convergent fashion. The synthesis involves copper(I)-mediated regioselective Grignard opening of (R)- and (S)-OTBS glycidyl ethers, base-induced elimination of β-alkoxy iodides followed by Cadiot–Chodkiewicz cross-coupling reaction as the key steps. D-(−)-Diethyl tartrate and both (R)- and (S)-OTBS glycidyl ethers were used as chiral pool starting materials for the construction of the crucial chiral ene-yne and chiral bromoalkyne fragments. Comparative analysis revealed that, out of all four possible (8E)-stereoisomers of panaxjapyne B, three isomers correspond to natural products.
item.dc:creator: Pamarthi Gangadhar, Sayini Ramakrishna, Pabbaraja Srihari
item.dc:title: Stereoselective Total Synthesis of All (8E)‐Stereoisomers of Panaxjapyne B

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Synthesis of Indoloquinolinone Derivatives via Organoiodine‐Catalyzed Highly Chemoselective C−H Amidation
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, Volume 29, Issue 19, 22 May 2026.
item.dc:description:
A novel synthetic approach for the synthesis of indoloquinolinones via organoiodine‐catalyzed intramolecular oxidative C−H amidation. The key factor in the success of this method is the introduction of a nitro group at the C5 position of the aromatic ring. This straightforward strategy offers several advantages, including mild reaction conditions, good tolerance of functional groups, and high chemoselectivity. In this work, this modification of substrates effectively reduces the electronic density of the indole, suppressing competitive side reactions and tuning the reactivity to ensure compatibility with complex oxidative conditions.
New catalytic approaches for the synthesis of indoloquinolinone derivatives have gained significant interest, as indoles are pivotal structural elements in biologically active molecules and natural products. Despite the availability of well‐established transition‐metal catalytic methods for constructing such important frameworks, the organocatalytic synthesis of indole‐based substrates is still underdeveloped and remains a formidable challenge. In this context, we have developed a highly chemoselective C−H amidation reaction catalyzed by organoiodine without dearomatization of indole‐fused reactants in a complex oxidative system. Notably, the introduction of a nitro group at the C5‐position of indole substantially enhances the reactivity of the substrates, thereby effectively accelerating the oxidative reaction under catalytic conditions. This highly selective transformation of diphenylindoles into indoloquinolinones offers a promising strategy for synthesizing diverse and bioactive indole alkaloids.
item.content:encoded:
A novel synthetic approach for the synthesis of indoloquinolinones via organoiodine-catalyzed intramolecular oxidative C−H amidation. The key factor in the success of this method is the introduction of a nitro group at the C5 position of the aromatic ring. This straightforward strategy offers several advantages, including mild reaction conditions, good tolerance of functional groups, and high chemoselectivity. In this work, this modification of substrates effectively reduces the electronic density of the indole, suppressing competitive side reactions and tuning the reactivity to ensure compatibility with complex oxidative conditions.


New catalytic approaches for the synthesis of indoloquinolinone derivatives have gained significant interest, as indoles are pivotal structural elements in biologically active molecules and natural products. Despite the availability of well-established transition-metal catalytic methods for constructing such important frameworks, the organocatalytic synthesis of indole-based substrates is still underdeveloped and remains a formidable challenge. In this context, we have developed a highly chemoselective C−H amidation reaction catalyzed by organoiodine without dearomatization of indole-fused reactants in a complex oxidative system. Notably, the introduction of a nitro group at the C5-position of indole substantially enhances the reactivity of the substrates, thereby effectively accelerating the oxidative reaction under catalytic conditions. This highly selective transformation of diphenylindoles into indoloquinolinones offers a promising strategy for synthesizing diverse and bioactive indole alkaloids.

item.dc:title: Synthesis of Indoloquinolinone Derivatives via Organoiodine‐Catalyzed Highly Chemoselective C−H Amidation

item.source: Wiley: European Journal of Organic Chemistry: Table of Contents
item.title: Carbene‐Catalyzed Enantioselective Synthesis of 1‐Oxyacylated‐2,4‐Disubstituted Cyclopentenes
item.description: European Journal of Organic Chemistry, Volume 29, Issue 19, 22 May 2026.
item.dc:description:
A carbene‐catalyzed enantioselective synthesis of multi‐functionalized oxyacylated cyclopentenes through [3 + 2] cycloaddition of α‐functionalized vinyl ketones and enals has been reported
Herein, we report the first carbene‐catalyzed enantioselective synthesis of multi‐functionalized oxyacylated cyclopentenes through [3 + 2] cycloaddition of α‐functionalized vinyl ketones and enals. The trisubstituted cyclopentenes were obtained with excellent enantioselectivity that could be readily converted to functionalized cyclopentenols.
item.content:encoded:
A carbene-catalyzed enantioselective synthesis of multi-functionalized oxyacylated cyclopentenes through [3 + 2] cycloaddition of α-functionalized vinyl ketones and enals has been reported


Herein, we report the first carbene-catalyzed enantioselective synthesis of multi-functionalized oxyacylated cyclopentenes through [3 + 2] cycloaddition of α-functionalized vinyl ketones and enals. The trisubstituted cyclopentenes were obtained with excellent enantioselectivity that could be readily converted to functionalized cyclopentenols.
